name: Craftiva
description: Management platform with a mobile app on iOS and Android: run sales, inventory, appointments, payroll and customers from your phone, with an AI layer that works on the business's real data.
highlights: Mobile app published on the App Store and Google Play, built for restaurants, salons, stores and clinics
POS with scanner, fractional inventory (kg → grams) and tickets straight to the kitchen
WhatsApp built in: appointment reminders, customer updates and a bot that books on its own
AI assistant that answers with the business's own data, and multi-company with per-module roles

name: Hallcyon Analyst
description: Business Intelligence that answers in natural language: connect your files, spreadsheets or databases, ask, and get analysis with charts and reports — without storing your data: the analysis lives in memory and is destroyed when the session ends.
highlights: Works like a RAG: it takes your information and turns it into the model's context — the AI doesn't answer from memory, it queries your real data and replies with that evidence
Chat that turns questions into SQL, charts and narrative, streamed over WebSocket
9 business agents: anomalies, churn, real margin, receivables risk, dormant inventory…
Connects CSV, Excel, Google Sheets, PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server and MongoDB
Privacy-first: raw data is never persisted; PDF reports ready to share

name: CraftBall
description: A football platform: the mobile app for the amateur game —pitches, matches with live scores, teams, tournaments and matchmaking— plus the 2026 World Cup predictions on the web, no install needed.
highlights: App published on the App Store and Google Play, with live matches over WebSocket
ELO rating and a FIFA-style player card that evolves with your real stats
Matchmaking between captains, tournaments and team management with QR invitations
2026 World Cup predictions: forecast all 104 matches, build your bracket and compete in private groups with your people

name: CraftDish
description: Multi-tenant SaaS to run a whole restaurant: table orders, real-time kitchen display, public QR menu and inventory — with AI that generates the menu content and a virtual waiter that serves the guest.
highlights: Real-time kitchen and waiter tray over WebSocket
Public QR menu with a conversational virtual waiter
Works without internet: offline mode and a local mesh between the restaurant's devices (WebRTC)
AI-powered HR: contracts, Colombian labor-law validation and pay-equity analysis
